implementationStatus: The project has scaffolding for Firebase/Firestore but relies on mocks for core logic.

missingFunctionality.description: src/firebase.ts exists but is not used in App.tsx. The app uses localStorage for requests and hardcoded state for user/vehicles.
missingFunctionality.remedy: Replace localStorage logic in App.tsx with Firestore queries and use useAuthState for real user profiles.

missingFunctionality.description: The current 'Searching' and 'Assigned' status changes are triggered by a manual button click in Provider Mode or a timer.
missingFunctionality.remedy: Implement Firestore listeners (onSnapshot) to update request status in real-time across different devices.

missingFunctionality.description: Firebase Auth logic in src/firebase.ts has been replaced with mock promises to facilitate local UI testing without external dependency.
missingFunctionality.remedy: Restore real Firebase Auth logic in src/firebase.ts by uncommenting imports/exports.

missingFunctionality.description: Firestore rules suggest a detailed vehicle model (mileage, year), but the UI only captures basic make/model/plate.
missingFunctionality.remedy: Update the 'Add New Vehicle' form to match the Firestore schema and persist data to the 'vehicles' collection.

missingFunctionality.description: MockMap is a CSS animation. navigator.geolocation is called but results are only shown as text strings.
missingFunctionality.remedy: Integrate a library like 'react-google-maps/api' or 'react-leaflet' to show actual provider movement on a real map.

missingFunctionality.description: Price calculations exist (₹) but there is no payment flow.
missingFunctionality.remedy: Integrate Razorpay or Stripe to handle transactions before confirming a request.

logic: Google Sign-In integration via src/firebase.ts
Email/Password authentication form
Persistent session management with Firebase AuthStateListener
Password reset and account creation flows
ui: Animated transitions for switching between Login and Signup
Responsive form layout with loading indicators (Loader2)

logic: Integration with a real map provider (Google Maps or Leaflet)
Real-time marker updates for provider and user location
Distance and ETA calculation using Direction API
Bounds fitting to show both user and provider simultaneously
ui: Clean, minimal map style (Dark mode compatible)
Custom SVG markers for different vehicle types
Smooth panning and zooming on status updates

logic: Integration with Razorpay, Stripe, or a sandbox for Indian market (UPI, Card, NetBanking)
Cost breakdown including base price, add-ons, and taxes
Secure transaction state management (Pending, Success, Failed)
Payment confirmation callback to trigger service dispatch
ui: Clear selection for payment methods with logos

logic: Real-time queue of incoming service requests from Firestore
Earnings analytics (Daily, Weekly, Monthly filters)
Profile management (Service specialty, vehicle type, documents)
Availability toggle (Online/Offline status)
ui: Quick action buttons for status updates (Accept, Arrived, Complete)
Earnings charts (using lightweight SVG or a library)

logic: Centralized handler for push and in-app notifications
Status update alerts (Provider assigned, 5 mins away, arrived)
Notification history log for auditing
Sound and vibration alerts for high-priority requests
ui: Consistent iconography matching the service type

logic: User and Provider management (Approve/Deactivate)
Service configuration (Price editing, icon management)
Global system status and incident report logs
Revenue monitoring and commission management
ui: Data tables with sorting and filtering
System health metrics (Active users, Pending jobs)

structuralRecommendations: Modularize App.tsx: The file is currently over 1000 lines. Split it into smaller components in src/components/.
Centralize State: Use a Context API or a store (Zustand/Redux) to manage shared data instead of passing props through 5 layers.
Add Environment Variables: Use .env for Firebase configuration instead of importing a JSON file directly for better security.
Implement Error Tracking: Integrate Sentry or a similar service to monitor runtime errors beyond the local ErrorBoundary.
